entities.name: Traditional Resume
entities.context: Described as a failed data structure lacking intrinsic authentication

entities.name: Intrinsic Authentication
entities.context: What resumes lack, making them vulnerable to fraud

entities.name: LLM-generated Fraud
entities.context: Zero-cost exploitation enabled by AI that produces high-signal-sounding false claims

entities.name: Multi-modal Consistency
entities.context: Verification approach requiring signals from different expressions to align

entities.name: Cheap Talk
entities.context: Game theory concept where signals have no cost to produce and provide no proof of quality

entities.name: Costly Signals
entities.context: Game theory concept representing signals that require investment to produce, anchoring trust

entities.name: Cross-verification
entities.context: Trust mechanism that verifies integrity through structural consistency across dimensions

entities.name: Triangle of Integrity
entities.context: Proposed model that synthesizes disparate signals (written, verbal, behavioral) to verify authenticity

entities.name: Top-of-funnel Filtering
entities.context: Hiring process stage that breaks down when relying on unauthenticated documents

entities.name: Game Theory
entities.context: Referenced field providing concepts like cheap talk and costly signals

relationships.context: Resume fails because it lacks intrinsic authentication
LLM fraud fully exploits the resume's authentication vulnerability
Proposed replacement verification approach for static resume claims
Opposing game theory concepts - trust must shift from cheap talk to costly signals

Proposed replacement system for deprecated resume-based hiring
